entry.title: Obsolete
Re: Changing condition supplements doesn't create a workl...
entry.content: In standard SAP, only gross price changes (or gross + supplement together) trigger ADA worklist entries. Condition supplements alone are not considered relevant fields. To change this, you’d need either customizing or an enhancement.

entry.title: 2026 China Individual Income Tax Withholding System Updates: Solution Details for Phase 2
entry.content:
Key Updates

SAP has released two-phase solutions for the 2026 China Individual Income Tax (IIT) withholding system updates:

  • Phase 1 (SAP Note 3710991): Manual operation approach
  • Phase 2 (SAP Note 3746570): Hybrid approach combining automatic calculation with manual configuration

If your organization has frequent employee turnover, numerous back pay scenarios for terminated employees, or wants to reduce manual maintenance efforts, Phase 2's automatic calculation approach will significantly improve efficiency.

1. Regulatory Changes Explained

1.1 Cumulative Basic Deduction Calculation Rule Optimization
Before the change:
The system automatically calculated cumulative basic deduction based on employee hiring month and tax period.

After the change:
Cumulative Basic Deduction = Current Period Basic Deduction + Prior Period Cumulative Basic Deduction

Business impact:
This change means employees must file continuously in the Golden Tax System to accumulate multi-period basic deductions. If filing is interrupted for any month (e.g., no salary paid), the system cannot accumulate previous basic deductions and must restart calculation from the current month.

1.2 New Special Additional Deduction Rules
When all three conditions are met simultaneously:

  • Termination date month < Tax period month
  • "Back pay after termination" is "Yes"
  • Back pay tax period month = Tax period month

The system treats the "back pay tax period month" as the "termination date" to calculate special additional deduction amounts.

SAP Standard Solution Support:
SAP's /4K* series wage types already support Year-to-Date (YTD) amount storage, meeting the new regulatory requirements.

2. Business Scenario Analysis

Scenario 1: Continuous Filing Employees

  • Regular monthly salary payments
  • Continuous filing in Golden Tax System
  • Can accumulate multi-period basic deductions

Scenario 2: Discontinuous Filing Employees

  • No salary paid in certain months (e.g., leave, unpaid leave)
  • Filing interrupted in Golden Tax System
  • Can only accumulate current period basic deduction; previous accumulation resets

Scenario 3: Terminated Employee Back Pay

  • Employee already terminated, subsequent back pay for previous months
  • Requires special handling of special additional deductions
  • Needs separate filing for different months

Scenario 4: Rehired Employees

  • Employee terminated and rehired
  • Requires cumulation reset
  • Needs correct tax entry date setup

3. Solution Details for Phase 2

3.1 Phase 2 Solution Provides Two Options
Option 1: Manual Operation Approach (Inherited from Phase 1)

  • Maintain tax entry date via IT0531
  • Maintain TXEYD parameter via IT0041 + Feature 28TDR
  • Complete manual control of each employee's calculation logic

Option 2: Automatic Calculation Approach

  • System automatically checks prior period payroll results
  • Automatically determines continuous filing status
  • Automatically calculates basic deduction
  • Automatically handles cumulation reset
  • Can still be manually overridden via IT0531/IT0041

3.2 Recommended Principle:

  • Prioritize Phase 2 automatic calculation to reduce 90% of manual maintenance work
  • Retain flexibility - automatic calculation can still be overridden via IT0531/IT0041
  • Phased implementation - first enable TACM parameter, test, then enable automatic calculation

4. Implementation Guide for Phase 2 Solution

4.1 Parameter Configuration
4.1.1 V_T596C View Configuration
Parameter Name: TACM
Function: Enable new cumulative basic deduction calculation logic
Configuration Steps:

  1. Transaction code: SM30
  2. View name: V_T596C
  3. Find TACM parameter
  4. Set effective date (recommended to start from a month where payroll has not been run)

Important Note: Once TACM parameter is enabled, it affects all employees' calculation logic. Recommend full validation in test system first.

4.1.2 Feature 28TCE Configuration
Feature 28TCE controls two switches:
Switch 1: Automatic Calculation Switch
Return Value Description Recommendation

AX

Enable automatic calculation (default)

Recommended

A

Disable automatic calculation

Only for special scenarios

Not set

Equivalent to disabled

Not recommended

Function: System automatically checks prior period payroll results, determines continuous filing status, automatically calculates basic deduction, and automatically handles cumulation reset.

Switch 2: Zero Filing Switch
Return Value Description Recommendation

ZX

Enable zero filing

Use for specific scenarios

Z

Disable zero filing (default)

Recommended

Not set

Equivalent to disabled

Recommended

4.2 Calculation Logic Details
4.2.1 Basic Deduction Calculation Formula
Basic Deduction = [ min(Tax Period Month, Termination Month) - max(Hiring Month, Tax Year January) + 1 ] × 5,000

4.2.2 Hiring Month Priority
Original Approach (Phase 1):

  1. IT0531 tax entry date (manual)
  2. Feature 28TDR parameter TXEYD + IT0041 record (manual)
  3. BADI: HRPAD00_ENTRY_LEAVE (automatic)
  4. Feature ENTRY parameter + IT0000/0001/0016/0041 records (automatic/manual)

New Approach (Phase 2 with automatic calculation enabled):

  1. IT0531 tax entry date (manual)
  2. Key requirement: IT0531 record must start from the current payroll period
    - Example: When running March payroll, IT0531 must start from 03/01/2026
    - Feature 28TDR parameter TXEYD + IT0041 record (manual)
  3. System automatic setting (automatic) - NEW
    - Discontinuous filing: tax entry date = current month start
    - Continuous filing: continue checking priority 4, 5
  4. BADI: HRPAD00_ENTRY_LEAVE (automatic)
  5. Feature ENTRY parameter + related infotype records (automatic/manual)

Key Change: Phase 2 adds "system automatic determination" logic at priority 3, which is the core of automatic calculation implementation.

4.2.3 Termination Month Priority
Original Approach (Phase 1):

  1. IT0531 tax termination date (manual)
  2. BADI: HRPAD00_ENTRY_LEAVE (automatic)
  3. Feature LEAVE parameter + IT0000/0001 records (automatic)

New Approach (Phase 2):

  1. IT0531 tax termination date (manual)
    - When automatic calculation is enabled, IT0531 must also start from current period
  2. If no priority 1 value exists, no termination month is generated
    - This means termination month is primarily controlled through manual IT0531 maintenance

4.2.4 Cumulation Reset Priority
Original Approach (Phase 1):

  1. IT0531 cumulation reset checkbox (manual, must start from current period)
  2. IT0531 tax group change (manual)

New Approach (Phase 2):

  1. IT0531 cumulation reset checkbox (manual, must start from current period)
  2. IT0531 tax group change (manual)
  3. NEW: System automatically determines discontinuous filing and resets (automatic)

Key Change: Phase 2 adds "system automatic determination of discontinuous filing" logic at priority 3, automatically handling cumulation reset.

4.3 Automatic Calculation Logic Details
4.3.1 Zero Filing Switch Determination
Switch Status: Enabled (ZX)

  • System behavior: Directly determines employee as continuous filing
  • Cumulation rule: Includes prior period + current period
  • Applicable scenario: Enterprises adopting full filing principle, filing even when no salary is paid

Switch Status: Disabled (Z or not set) - Recommended

  • System behavior: Checks prior period payroll result status
  • Cumulation rule: Determined based on actual filing status
  • Applicable scenario: Standard scenario for most enterprises

4.3.2 Prior Period Payroll Result Status Determination
System determines filing status by checking prior period payroll run type:
Run Type Determination Condition Filing Status

Regular Run

In Period = For Period

Filed

Retro Run

In Period ≠ For Period

Not Filed

Off-cycle Run

Payment Date within prior month Payroll Period

Filed

Correction Run

Non-BB record and For Period within prior month Payroll Period

Filed

4.3.3 Impact of Continuous vs Discontinuous Filing
Cumulation Item Continuous Filing Discontinuous Filing

Basic Deduction (new rule)

Accumulate multi-period

Current period only

Special Additional Deduction (termination back pay new rule)

Accumulate multi-period

Accumulate multi-period

Key Understanding:

  • Basic deduction is affected by continuous filing
  • Special additional deduction is NOT affected by continuous filing (due to YTD storage in /4K* series wage types)

5. Typical Scenario Examples

Scenario 1: Active Employee with Continuous Filing
Background:

  • Employee A, hired January 2025
  • Regular monthly salary payments
  • Phase 2 solution enabled January 2026

Configuration:

  • TACM parameter: Enabled (from January 2026)
  • Feature 28TCE: AX (automatic calculation enabled)
  • IT0531: No special maintenance needed

System Behavior:
Month Payroll Result System Determination Basic Deduction

2026-01

Regular Run

Continuous filing

5,000

2026-02

Regular Run

Continuous filing

10,000 (5,000 + 5,000)

2026-03

Regular Run

Continuous filing

15,000 (10,000 + 5,000)

Result: System automatically accumulates, no manual maintenance needed

Scenario 2: Employee with No Salary in Certain Month (Discontinuous Filing)
Background:

  • Employee B, hired January 2025
  • No salary paid in February 2026 due to leave
  • Salary payment resumed March 2026

Configuration:

  • TACM parameter: Enabled
  • Feature 28TCE: AX (automatic calculation enabled)
  • IT0531: No special maintenance needed

System Behavior:
Month Payroll Result System Determination Basic Deduction

2026-01

Regular Run

Continuous filing

5,000

2026-02

No Payroll Run

--

--

2026-03

Regular Run

Discontinuous filing (detected no prior result)

5,000 (reset, current period only)

2026-04

Regular Run

Continuous filing

10,000 (5,000 + 5,000)

Result: System automatically detects interruption and resets, March restarts from 5,000

Scenario 3: Terminated Employee Back Pay
Background:

  • Employee C, terminated January 31, 2026
  • Back pay for December 2025 year-end bonus in March 2026

Configuration:

  • TACM parameter: Enabled
  • Feature 28TCE: AX (automatic calculation enabled)
  • IT0531: Requires manual maintenance of tax termination date and tax entry date

Operation Steps:

  1. Maintain IT0531 record
    - Delimit date: 12/31/2025
    - Tax Entry Date: 12/01/2025
    - Tax Termination Date: 12/31/2025
  2. Run March 2026 payroll
    - System recognizes as back pay after termination
    - Special additional deduction calculated based on December 2025
  3. Golden Tax System filing
    - "Back pay after termination": Select "Yes"
    - "Back pay tax period month": 2025-12

Result: Correctly handles termination back pay scenario, special additional deduction calculated based on back pay month

Scenario 4: Rehired Employee
Background:

  • Employee D, terminated June 30, 2025
  • Rehired March 1, 2026

Configuration:

  • TACM parameter: Enabled
  • Feature 28TCE: AX (automatic calculation enabled)
  • IT0531: Requires manual maintenance of new tax entry date

Operation Steps:

  1. Maintain IT0531 record (before running March 2026 payroll)
    - Create new record, start date: 03/01/2026
    - Tax Entry Date: 03/01/2026
    - Do not check Cumulation Reset (system will automatically reset)
  2. Run March 2026 payroll
    - System detects new tax entry date
    - Automatically resets cumulation, starts calculation from March

System Behavior:
Month Payroll Result System Determination Basic Deduction

2026-03

Regular Run

New hire (automatic reset)

5,000

2026-04

Regular Run

Continuous filing

10,000 (5,000 + 5,000)

Result: System automatically recognizes rehire and resets cumulation

6. Implementation Recommendations

✅Recommended Practices

  • Prioritize automatic calculation (Option 2)
  • Can still override results via IT0531/IT0041 under automatic calculation
  • Tax Termination Date primarily maintained manually via IT0531
  • Flexible configuration based on calculation formula and switch status

⚠️Important Notes

  • IT0531 records must start from current payroll period (when automatic calculation is enabled)
  • TACM parameter affects all employees once enabled
  • Recommend full validation in test system first
  • Zero filing switch (ZX) only for specific scenarios

❌Common Mistakes

  • IT0531 record dates incorrect (not starting from current period)
  • Forgetting to configure Feature 28TCE
  • Enabling directly in production system without testing
  • Confusing continuous filing with zero filing concepts

7. Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: Can Phase 1 and Phase 2 coexist?
Answer: No. Once TACM parameter is enabled, all employees will use the new calculation logic. However, Phase 2 includes all manual operation functions from Phase 1, so Phase 2 is an enhanced version of Phase 1.

Q2: Can I use automatic calculation for some employees and manual maintenance for others?
Answer: Yes. After enabling TACM + Feature 28TCE (AX), system defaults to automatic calculation. For employees requiring manual control, maintain IT0531 tax entry date to override automatic calculation results (higher priority).

Q3: When should I use the zero filing switch (ZX)?
Answer: Only when your enterprise adopts "full filing" principle, meaning filing in Golden Tax System even when no salary is paid. For most enterprises, recommend disabling this switch (default value Z) and letting the system determine based on actual payroll results.

Q4: What happens if employee filing is interrupted in Golden Tax System but salary is paid continuously in SAP?
Answer: If using Phase 2 automatic calculation, system will detect continuous payroll results and determine as "continuous filing", accumulating multi-period basic deductions. However, this may be inconsistent with actual Golden Tax System filing status. Recommendations:

  • If filing is actually interrupted in Golden Tax System, manually maintain IT0531 tax entry date to reset cumulation
  • Or ensure through business process that SAP salary payment remains consistent with Golden Tax filing

Q5: Do I still need to maintain IT0531 tax entry date and tax termination date in Phase 2?
Answer:

  • Automatic calculation scenarios: Most cases don't require maintenance, system handles automatically
  • Scenarios requiring manual maintenance:
    - Terminated employee back pay (need to set tax termination date)
    - Rehired employees (need to set new tax entry date)
    - Special adjustment scenarios (override automatic calculation results)

Q6: If we're already using Phase 1, how do we upgrade to Phase 2?
Answer:

  1. Apply SAP Note 3746570 in test system
  2. Configure Feature 28TCE (AX) to enable automatic calculation
  3. Full testing in test system
  4. Apply to production system after validation

 

Related Reference Articles
Understanding the Regulatory Change of Updates to Individual Income Tax Withholding System in China - Phase 1

 

Resources and Support
For more detailed information about regulatory changes released by SAP Globalization, and further collaboration and support on Greater China HCM localization, please visit the links provided below:

  • Regulatory Change Manager
  • Greater China - Human Capital Management Localization

 

Disclaimer
SAP is not a law firm and is not able to provide any legal advice.

entry.title: Additional Account Assignments in Manual Accruals
entry.content:
1. In Utilization and Release Postings (UP, RP)

Accrual and Utilization Posting Logic 

  • Accruals: Typically posted as a debit to a cost account, which usually requires additional account assignments such as cost center or internal order. 
  • Utilization Postings: Typically posted as a credit to a cost account. 

Default Behavior: 

  • The Accrual Engine posts the utilization posting using the same additional account assignment (e.g., cost center, internal order) specified in the accrual object. 
  • This ensures that the utilization posting matches the additional account assignment used for the accruals. 

Importance: 

  • This logic is crucial when the actual cost posting (e.g., supplier invoice) is assigned to a different account than that of the accruals, ensuring consistency and accuracy in financial reporting.

2. Utilization Postings: Time-Dependent Account Assignments 

  • Special case: Assume several accrual postings were done with different additional account assignments (cost center, internal order etc.). 
  • This can occur if the additional account assignment was changed between the accrual postings. 
  • The utilization posting will then use the additional account assignment that is currently entered/valid in the accrual object.

3. Late Utilization Postings: 

  • Late Utilization Postings can only occur if an accrual closing frequency is specified in the customizing of the accrual item type. 
  • The primary use case for an accrual closing frequency is 'Annually', to distinguish between utilization and release postings across fiscal years. 

Annual Use Case: 

  • Late utilization postings indicate that accruals were posted in the previous fiscal year. 
  • During the fiscal year change, the G/L balance carryforward run clears the balances of all profit accounts. Thus, all costs posted to a cost center (e.g., cost center 1) in the old year are reset to zero in the new year. 
  • Only the balance on the accrual account is carried forward, as it is a balance sheet account. 

Budget Impact: 

  • If actual costs in the new year (e.g., an invoice) are posted to a different cost center (e.g., cost center 2), a standard utilization posting crediting the original cost center (cost center 1) would increase its budget in the new year. This is because the debit posting from the old year is missing in the new year for cost center 1. 

Default Behavior: 

  • In Late Utilization (UL) postings, the Accrual Engine defaults to posting the credit to the new cost center (cost center 2) instead of the original cost center (cost center 1). 
  • This behavior differs from "normal" utilization postings (UP) and release postings, as explained in the corresponding documentation. 

 4. Release Postings: 

  • Similar to utilization and late utilization postings, release postings are typically posted as credits to a cost account. 
  • The logic for posting release of accruals is similar to that of utilization postings, with one key difference: there is no actual posting (such as a supplier invoice) from which to derive the additional account assignment. 
  • For both "normal" release postings (transaction types RP or FP) and late release postings (transaction types RL or FL), the additional account assignment is derived from the accrual object using the posting date of the release posting. 

5. Late Release Postings: 

  • Late release postings occur when an accrual closing frequency (e.g., Annually) is used, and accruals posted in a previous closing period (e.g., fiscal year) are being released. 
  • Similar to the logic for late utilization postings, this approach prevents the creation of a budget on the "old" cost center (e.g., cost center 1) in the new fiscal year due to the credit posting from the release of accruals. 

Issue: 

  • With late release postings, a credit is posted to cost center 1, which increases the budget in the new year. 
  • If this budget increase is not desired, a Business Add-In (BAdI) can be implemented to derive a different cost center, such as a dummy cost center. 

Solution: 

  • The account assignment can be changed by implementing the BAdI BADI_ACE_DOCUMENT_ASGNMT_CUST when deriving them from the accrual object for postings. 

Changing Default Account Assignment Behavior in Manual Accruals

entry.title: Print via output control indicator - Goods movement
entry.content:
I am using public cloud. I already set all the parameter in Output parameter determination.

However, in test inviroment,  I have to manually check in "Print via output control", then the output is triggered. But in dev environment, I do not need to check manually, when I click Post, the system will check for me and output is automaticaly trigger. But I can not find where is the difference between two enviroment. I want it to be auto to avoid user forgetting to check manually. Please help if you know.

Thank you very much.

entry.title: I want to know how to map a QueryManageSiteLogisticTask to ManageSiteLogisticsTaskIn
entry.content:
Hey, 
I'm trying to create an automation of managing pick tasks in sap bydesign, using cloud integration iflow. I need to use the information from the next web service (Query Site Logistics Task) and insert the data to the web service ManageSiteLogisticsTask while supporting a split operation for different identified stock id values for a certain product and line.

The query response does not contain all the information about the split. I have a table which im saving in the iflow as a property, and this table contains the info about the lines that need to split. 
I have a few questions about rules and parameters of these web services, which I was not sure about from the documentation:

1. Assume I have one Sales Order ID and one task (pick task) which contains all the items for that order.  Does the element "OperationActivity"   include the MaterialInput/output for all of the lines in the order? 
2. I saw in a few payload examples of the Manage Site Logistics Tasks Web Service, that when it's a Pick task, they did not insert MaterialInput values. I thought that it's mandatory to insert something (just copy the same values in both output and input) as the example here. So I need to know if it's necessary or not. 
3. The example in the previous section has the ClearIdentifiedStockIndicator field wrapped in another MaterialOutput element. I want to know if it's the only way, because according to the wsdl the code looks like this :

<MaterialOutput>
<!--Optional:-->
<MaterialOutputUUID schemeID="?" schemeAgencyID="?">?</MaterialOutputUUID>
<!--Optional:-->
<ProductID schemeID="?" schemeAgencyID="?">?</ProductID>
<!--Optional:-->
<SourceLogisticsAreaIDPostSplit>?</SourceLogisticsAreaIDPostSplit>
<!--Optional:-->
<TargetLogisticsAreaID>?</TargetLogisticsAreaID>
<!--Optional:-->
<ActualQuantity unitCode="?">?</ActualQuantity>
<!--Optional:-->
<IdentifiedStockID schemeID="?" schemeVersionID="?" schemeAgencyID="?">?</IdentifiedStockID>
<!--Optional:-->
<ProductRequirementSpecificationID schemeID="?" schemeAgencyID="?">?</ProductRequirementSpecificationID>
<!--Optional:-->
<InventoryRestrictedIndicator>?</InventoryRestrictedIndicator>
<!--Optional:-->
<LogisticsDeviationReasonCode listAgencyID="?">?</LogisticsDeviationReasonCode>
<!--Optional:-->
<SplitIndicator>?</SplitIndicator>
<!--Zero or more repetitions:-->
<LogisticsPackageOutput>
<!--Optional:-->
<LogisticsUnitUUID schemeID="?" schemeAgencyID="?">?</LogisticsUnitUUID>
<!--Optional:-->
<LogisticsUnitID schemeID="?" schemeVersionID="?" schemeAgencyID="?">?</LogisticsUnitID>
<!--Optional:-->
<LogisticsUnitQuantity unitCode="?">?</LogisticsUnitQuantity>
</LogisticsPackageOutput>
<!--Optional:-->
<AutomaticIdentification>
<!--Zero or more repetitions:-->
<BarCodeText>?</BarCodeText>
</AutomaticIdentification>
<!--Optional:-->
<SerialNumber>
<!--Zero or more repetitions:-->
<SerialID>?</SerialID>
</SerialNumber>
<!--Optional:-->
<ClearIdentifiedStockIDIndicator>?</ClearIdentifiedStockIDIndicator>

</MaterialOutput>

meaning I should insert the MaterialOutput values and the ClearIdentifiedStockID value under the same scope.
4. I wanted to know if its ok to confirm only part of the lines in a pick task, by sending the payload only with the MaterialOutput of the Sales Order Lines which have been confirmed, and then call the web service again to finish confirming the rest? 
5. If I go by the structure of Manage Site Logistics Task payload I need to understand the hirarchy of the values in the response of QuerySiteLogisticsTask. And then I need to save these values in the table and know exactly which rows relate to which UUID values. I want to know exactly the ratio and predecessor-successor relations between the UUID fields when I have for example one order, one task, two lines, and the first line is split to 2 quantities by the identifiedstockid.

Any suggestions on how to solve this problem or partially help me will be really useful, thanks!

entry.title: Posting Summarization in Accruals
entry.content:
Before Release 2020: 

  • The transaction ACEPOSTINGRUN aimed to minimize the number of documents by summarizing them as much as possible. 

From Release 2020 Onwards: 

  • The transaction ACEPOSTINGRUN offers more flexibility by allowing summarization of posting documents at different levels: maximum summarization, accrual subobject level, and accrual item level. 
  • This enhanced flexibility enables customers to view the posting results and perform true reversals of individual documents. 
  • Previously, reversals were done by posting the same amount on the opposite credit/debit side, as reversing individual documents often affected multiple accrual subobjects. 
  • With the ability to post for a single accrual subobject or even a single accrual item, documents can now be truly reversed. 

IMG Activity: 

  • To define the aggregation level, use the IMG activity in transaction ACEIMG: 
  • Path: Accruals Management -> Accrual Postings -> Define Posting Summarization 

Test Run Mode in Transaction ACEPOSTINGRUN: 

  • When the transaction ACEPOSTINGRUN is executed in test run mode, the summarization customizing settings are disregarded. Instead, postings are summarized at the accrual items and posting types level. 
  • The rationale behind this is that error messages generated during the posting run are attached to each document. If documents are created for multiple accrual subobjects, error messages cannot be linked to the corresponding accrual subobjects, making it difficult to identify the root cause. 
  • This feature was introduced in release 2020 and has been backported to release 1809 SP5 and release 1909 SP3 through Note 2912042. 

Account Assignment 

  • Previously, account assignment fields were present in the posting run result list, but most of these fields were empty because only the account assignment from the accrual account (a balance sheet account) was displayed. 
  • This behavior has been changed: now, the account assignment from the offsetting account (usually a P&L account) is shown. 
  • This update is available in release 2020 and has been downported to release 1809 SP4 and release 1909 SP3 through Note 2893536. 

Sign of Amounts 

  • Previously, in the posting run result list, the Accrual Amount was shown with a negative sign, while the Remaining Amount and Accumulated Amount were shown with a positive sign. 
  • The Accrual Amount followed the value in the posting document, while the Remaining Amount and Accumulated Amount followed the value in the Calculated Accruals in the Tree UI (Transaction ACACTREE02). This inconsistency sometimes caused confusion for end users. 
  • The new behavior is that the Accrual Amount, Remaining Amount, and Accumulated Amount are all shown with a negative sign, providing consistency. 
  • This update is available in release 2020. 

Quantity Only Postings are Enabled in Two Cases: 

  1. Change in Accrual Items: 
  • When the quantity is changed in Accrual Items, an opening posting will be triggered. 
  • The resulting posting document will contain only the updated quantity, with the amount fields set to 0. 
  1. Differences in Quantities in ACEPOSTINGRUN: 
  • During the ACEPOSTINGRUN, if there is a difference between the balance quantity and the posted quantity for other types of postings (e.g., periodic posting), a posting document will be created. 
  • This document will contain only the differing quantity, with the amount fields set to 0. 

Availability: 

  • This feature is available in release 2020 and has been downported to release 1809 SP5 and release 1909 SP3 through Notes 2926918 and 2936548. 

Use Cases: 

  1. Contract Deactivation: 
  • A contract is closed in December 2019 and reaches the admin department in late January. 
  • The contract needs to be deactivated. FI-Periods may be closed, but the posting must occur in the next available period. 
  1. Backdated Contract Change: 
  • A contract change is made in June, but it is backdated to April (a common practice due to warranty issues, complaints, etc.). 
  • FI-periods are closed, but the posting must be made in the next available period. 

Support in Accrual Engine: 

  • Before release 1809, these scenarios were not supported in the Accrual Engine. 
  • They are now supported in the S/4HANA Accrual Engine. 
  • Supported actions include opening posting, periodic posting, and release posting. 

IMG Activity: 

  • To define this behavior in transaction ACEIMG: 
  • Path: Accruals Management -> Accrual Postings -> Settings for Accrual Postings on Application Level 

Availability: 

  • This feature is available in release 2020 and has been downported to release 1809 SP4 and release 1909 FPS2 via Note 2869043.

entry.title: Accrual Closing Frequency
entry.content:
The Accrual Engine enables a generic distinction between the last and current fiscal year for utilization and release postings. For instance, when accruals are utilized through a posting of actual costs, such as a supplier invoice, it may be necessary to differentiate whether the utilized accruals were created in the previous or the current fiscal year.

If accruals were created in the previous fiscal year, it means they were posted by the Accrual Engine with an Accrual Value Date that falls within the former fiscal year. Note that when posting accruals, the accrual value date typically matches the posting date.

Background: Accruals posted at the end of the last fiscal year reduce the company's profit. Consequently, the company typically pays lower taxes. To demonstrate to an auditor that the accruals from the previous year were indeed necessary, it is essential to show in reporting:

  • The portion of last year's accruals utilized in the subsequent fiscal year, and
  • The portion of last year's accruals that had to be released due to non-use in the next fiscal year.

The Accrual Engine can be configured to use different transaction types (UP and UL) for posting utilizations of accruals from the last fiscal year and the current fiscal year. This allows for the distinction of corresponding journal entry line items in reporting. This capability is not limited to the utilization of accruals; it also applies to the manual release (RP and RL) and automatic release (FP and FL) of accruals.

For example, the release of accruals from the last fiscal year can be posted with a different transaction type compared to the release of accruals from the current fiscal year. Technically, these different transaction types correspond to different posting schemas (SAP_ACE_UP, SAP_ACE_UL, SAP_ACE_FP, SAP_ACE_FL). These posting schemas result in different values for the field “Subledger-Specific Line Item Type” (field SLALITTYPE in database table ACDOCA) in the line items of the journal entries.

When postings are not made online but through the periodic accrual posting run, different journal entry types (field BLART) in the header of the corresponding journal entries can be used.

The journal entry type is defined in the IMG activity "Assign Accrual Item Types to Journal Entry Types and Posting Schemas." These fields can be used for evaluation in reporting.

 

The Accrual Engine supports not only distinguishing between the former and current fiscal year for accrual utilization and release postings but also accommodates other frequencies such as quarters or even fiscal periods. This frequency is referred to as the Accruals Closing Frequency.

Consequently, the accruals closing frequency defines Accrual Closing Periods:

  • If "Annually" is entered as the accruals closing frequency, each fiscal year represents an accrual closing period.
  • If "Quarterly" is entered as the accruals closing frequency, each quarter represents an accrual closing period.

The accruals closing frequency is configured in the IMG activity "Define Accrual Item Types" (see screenshot for reference).

 

 

Since the accrual closing frequency is not limited to the fiscal year, the transaction types have "generic" names. Transaction types that represent the utilization or release of accruals from a previous accrual closing period include the word "Late" in their names. They do not use terms like "Former Fiscal Year" because the accrual closing frequency can be annual, quarterly, or another period.

Accruals utilization postings use the following transaction types:

  • UP (Utilization Posting): If the posting utilizes accruals from the current fiscal year.
  • UL (Late Utilization Posting): If the posting utilizes accruals from a previous accrual closing period.

Accruals release postings use the following transaction types:

  • FP (Automatic Release Posting, also called Closing Posting or Final Posting): If the posting releases accruals from the current fiscal year.
  • FL (Late Automatic Release Posting): If the posting releases accruals from a previous accrual closing period.
  • RP (Manual Release Posting): If the posting releases accruals from the current fiscal year.
  • RL (Late Manual Release Posting): If the posting releases accruals from a previous accrual closing period.

 

The accrual value date is populated only in journal entry line items created by the Accrual Engine. By default, the accrual value date is the same as the posting date. However, there are exceptions for Late Utilization (UL) and Late Release (RL, FL) postings.

In these exceptional cases, to indicate the accrual closing period from which accruals are utilized or released, the Accrual Engine sets the accrual value date to the last day of that accrual closing period.

 

Accruals Closing Frequency is Optional

If you do not need to differentiate between utilization or release postings for accruals from the previous or current year, you can leave the accrual closing frequency field blank in the IMG activity "Define Accrual Item Types."

When not using an accruals closing frequency, you also do not need to assign the transaction types UL, RL, and FL to posting schemas in the IMG activity "Assign Accrual Item Types to Journal Entry Types and Posting Schemas."

However, if you are using an accruals closing frequency, you must assign the transaction types UL, RL, and FL to posting schemas in the IMG activity "Assign Accrual Item Types to Journal Entry Types and Posting Schemas."

 

In the Plan minus Actual approach, three accrual item types are relevant:

  • PLNCST for planned costs
  • ACTCST for actual costs (invoices or goods receipts)
  • ACCRL_PMA for accruals, calculated as planned costs minus actual costs

You can only specify an accrual closing frequency in the accrual item type for accruals (ACCRL_PMA). It is neither supported nor allowed to specify an accrual closing frequency in the other two accrual item types for actual and planned costs (PLNCST and ACTCST).

This rule also applies to other utilization scenarios (ACCRL_PER, ACCRL_STD):

  • The accrual closing frequency can only be specified in the ACCRL* accrual item types.

entry.title: Effective Start Date Validation in Payment Information - SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Introduction :

Every time after the payroll run, One of my Clients faced a recurring issue and this recurring issue was causing last minute hassles and inconvenience to the Business. The Issue is the maintenance of Bank Details in the Payment Information Portlet mid of the month by the HR Team as they don't notice the effective start date which by defaults sets to today. Maintenance of mid-month Bank details will impact all the payroll reports, and the reports will show inconsistent values , which will then impact reconciliation and so on..

How a simple and effective Business Rule solved this challenge?

I noticed this issue become so common over the days and as a consultant it's my core duty to understand and resolve the business pain point. So I configured a simple and effective rule which will solve this recurring issue and any issues that follow by.

The Business Rule :

Base Object : Payment Information

Purpose : Evaluate / Validate

This simple rule here does the magic of validating the effective start and throwing error message when maintained mid of the month. There's a little catch and purpose behind using the previous effective validation here , the reason is explained below

We don't have an option in Business Rule for MDF's to have it triggered only for certain scenarios which means this will also trigger in the new hire UI. Imagine an employee joining mid of the month 03rd May 2026 , the validation kicks and throws error , ID is skipped and issue occurs.

To avoid this rule being triggered for the new hires , Previous Effective dated record is not equal to null condition is added in the IF condition.

Conclusion

This simple validation rule effectively prevents mid-month changes to bank details, eliminating inconsistencies in payroll reports and reducing reconciliation issues.

It also highlights an important lesson:
Every validation or enhancement should be carefully designed with edge cases in mind and thoroughly tested across all scenarios.

entry.title: Durch die Blume gesagt - mein virtueller Garten: Einheit 1 - Lektion 1
entry.content:
An English version of this lesson is available at: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/get-coding-with-snap-building-up-to-ai/create-your-own-virtual-flower-garden 

 

Videolektion "mein virtueller Garten"

In dieser Lektion wirst du die Snap! Programmiersprache und Entwicklungsumgebung kennenlernen. Mithilfe von Schleifen wirst du aus Blütenblättern Blumen kreieren und aus diesen Blumen eine ganze Blumenwiese. 

Folge den Schritten aus dem Video in Snap!. Die Aufgaben bauen teilweise auf das Projekt aus dem Video auf. 

 

 

Du bist dran!

  • Zeichne ein Kostüm für dein Objekt mit dem Kostüm-Editor. Teste dann die Grafik-Effekt Blöcke aus der “Aussehen” Kategorie. Experimentiere mit verschiedenen Effekten und verschiedenen Eingabewerten für die Effekt-Stärke.
    Blöcke, mit denen die Grafikeffekte gesetzt und geändert werden können.
  • Erweitere das Skript für deine Blumenwiese so, dass es zusätzliche Blumen malt, die Kleiner sind und nur 5 Blütenblätter haben.
  • Versuche, einen interaktiven Garten zu machen. Verwende die folgenden Blöcke, um ein Skript zu schreiben, das eine Blume an der Position des Mauszeigers stempelt, immer wenn die Maustaste gedrückt wird.
    mit dem "Wenn _" Startblock, dem "Maustaste gedrückt" Prädikat und dem "gehe zu" Befehl kann eine interaktive Version des Projekts erstellt werden.
  • Experimentiere und probiere weitere Blöcke aus.

Hier findest du die Aufgaben auch zum Herunterladen:

  • als PDF: https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/Hccj75g?rc=10&doi=SAP1292797 
  • als editierbare docx Version:  https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/zCRKsKy?rc=10&doi=SAP1292799

Lösungsvorschläge für Einheit 1 - Lektion 1 findest du in dieser Collection.

 

 

Zusammenfassung - das hast du diese Lektion gelernt

Bausteine dieser Lektion:

Bewegt dein Objekt an eine bestimmte Position, basierend auf der ausgewählten Option im Eingabefeld. Diese Stelle kann beispielsweise Zufall, der Mauszeiger, die Mitte der Bühne oder ein anderes Objekt sein. 
Verändert die Größe von Objekten relativ oder absolut.
Wendet den im ersten Eingabefeld ausgewählten Grafikeffekt auf das Objekt an. Die Stärke des Effekts hängt ab vom Wert im zweiten Eingabefeld. Der "setze" Block verwendet absolute Werte, der "ändere" Block benutzt den aktuellen Grafikeffekt als Referenz. 
Zieht dem Objekt das nächste Kostüm an. Wechselt durch alle Kostüme des Objekts, der Richtungsanzeiger wird ausgelassen. 
Stempelt das aktuelle Kostüm deines Objekts auf die Bühne.
Entfernt alles, was momentan auf die Bühne gemalt oder gestempelt ist.
Schleife, die die Blöcke in ihrem c-förmigen Eingabefeld so oft wiederholt, wie im ersten Eingabefeld angegeben. 

 

Die Snap! Programmiersprache und Entwicklungsumgebung

Du kannst auf die Snap! Programmierumgebung im Browser zugreifen unter snap.berkeley.edu -> Run Snap!
Oder du kannst diesen direkten Link verwenden: snap.berkeley.edu/run.

Übersicht über die Snap! Entwicklungsumgebung

Snap! ist eine block-basierte Programmiersprache, das heißt, dass du programmierst, indem du im Skriptbereich (7) Programmeinheiten - die Blöcke - mit Drag and Drop zu sogenannten Skripten zusammenfügst. 

Du findest mögliche Blöcke für dein Program in der Palette (5), dort sind sie in verschiedene farblich codierte Kategorien sortiert (6).

Die Blöcke sind thematisch in verschiedene Kategorien gruppiert. Kategorien und darin enthaltene Blöcke sind farblich codiert.

 

In Snap! gibt es 3 verschiedene Typen von Blöcken - Befehle, Funktionen (Reporter) und Prädikate: 

Befehle sehen ein bisschen aus wie Puzzleteile und tun etwas. Reporter haben eine ovale Form und berichten einen Wert. Sie können als Eingaben für andere Blöcke verwendet werden. Prädikate sind besondere Reporter-Blöcke. Sie sind sechseckig und können nur "wahr" oder "falsch" berichten. 

 

Manche Blöcke in Snap! haben Eingaben (auch Parameter genannt). Das heißt, du kannst durch deinen Eingabewert beeinflussen, was genau der Block macht. Es gibt Eingabefelder, bei denen du aus einer bestimmten Auswahl von Eingabemöglichkeiten auswählen kannst (z.B. die Liste der Grafikeffekte). Bei anderen Eingaben kannst du selbst Werte auswählen (z.B. die Distanz, die sich ein Objekt vorwärts bewegt, wenn der "gehe" Block ausgeführt wird). Diese Eingabefelder sind weiß hinterlegt. 

Der ausgeführte Grafikeffekt kann aus einem Dropdown-Menü ausgewählt werden. Die Stärke des Effekts oder die Distanz, die mit dem "gehe"-Block überwunden wird, kann durch die eingegebenen Zahlen verändert werden.

Skripte können Reaktionen von Objekten auslösen, die auf der Bühne (3) angezeigt werden. Im Objektbereich (4) bekommst du einen Überblick über alle Objekte, die in deinem Projekt vorhanden sind. Dort kannst du auch neue Objekte hinzufügen. 

 

Wenn du ein neues Objekt erzeugst, sieht es immer aus, wie eine Pfeilspitze (Richtungsanzeiger). Du kannst deinem Objekt aber ein individuelles Aussehen verleihen, indem du es ein sogenanntes Kostüm anziehen lässt. 

Du kannst Kostüme auf verschiedene Arten erzeugen, z.B. mit dem Kostümeditor, auf den du über den "Kostüme" Tab deines Objekts zugreifen kannst. 

Der Kostümeditor verfügt über verschiedene Werkzeuge, mit denen du ein Kostüm erstellen kannst. Das Freizeichen-Werkzeug lässt dich beliebige Formen erzeugen, andere Werkzeuge lassen dich voreingestellte Formen zeichnen. 
Du kannst die Pinselstärke anpassen oder deine Lieblingsfarbe über die Farbauswahl ändern. Mit dem Eimer (Füll-Werkzeug) kannst du geschlossene Formen mit einer Farbe ausmalen. Falls dir nicht gefällt, was du gemalt hast, kannst du den letzten Schritt mit dem "rückgängig" Knopf zurücknehmen oder auf "wische" klicken, um von vorne zu beginnen. 

Vom Kostüme-Tab hast du Zugriff auf den Kostüm-Editor, mit dem du eigene Kostüme für dein Objekt zeichnen kannst.

 

Projekte speichern

Wenn dir dein Projekt gefällt, kannst du es im Menü-Bereich (1) speichern. Du kannst dich entweder für ein Cloud-Konto registrieren oder deine Projekte lokal speichern, indem du sie als *.xml-Datei in den Downloads-Ordner deines Browsers exportierst. 

Klicke auf "Sichern als...", wenn du ein Projekt zum ersten Mal speicherst. Dann wähle entweder "Computer" oder "Cloud" aus, je nachdem, ob du dein Projekt lokal auf deinem Computer oder in der Cloud speichern möchtest. Gib dem Projekt einen aussagekräftigen Namen, so dass du es später unter diesem Namen wieder finden kannst. Dann klicke auf den "OK"-Knopf um das Projekt zu speichern. 

Wenn du das Projekt bereits unter dem gewünschten Namen gespeichert hast, kannst du die "Speichern" Option auswählen, um die aktuelle Version des Projekts in der Cloud zu überschreiben oder eine neue Version des Projekts herunterzuladen basierend darauf, was beim letzten Speichern gemacht wurde.  

Möchtest du ein Projekt laden, kannst du auf "Öffnen..." klicken, und dann zu einem Projekt in der Cloud oder auf deinem Computer navigieren, um es zu öffnen. 

Klicke auf das Datei-Menü und wähle "Speichern als...", um das Projekt zum ersten Mal zu speichern. Dann wähle den Speicherort (Cloud oder Computer), gib dem Projekt einen Namen und klicke "OK", um es zu speichern.

 

Und jetzt?

Das wars mit Einheit 1 - Lektion 1: Durch die Blume gesagt - mein virtueller Garten

  • Zur nächsten Lektion des Kurses geht es hier: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-young-thinkers-program-blog-posts/um-die-ecke-gedacht-geometrische-formen-einheit-1-lektion-2/ba-p/14389444
  • Zur Übersichtsseite von Einheit 1 geht es hier: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-young-thinkers-program-blog-posts/einheit-1-bausteine-programmieren... 
  • Zur Übersichtsseite des gesamten Kurses geht es hier: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-young-thinkers-program-blog-posts/programmieren-mit-snap-building-u... 
  • Eine Englische Version des Kurses, bei der ein Zertifikat erworben werden kann, findet sich hier: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/get-coding-with-snap-building-up-to-ai 

 

Dieses Material ist veröffentlicht unter der CC 4.0 Lizenz durch das Snap! Team bei SAP

entry.title: Manual and Automatic Release Postings
entry.content:
Process steps and usage

  1. Creation: An accrual object is created. 
  2. Posting: Accruals are posted, either through an opening posting (IP?) or a periodic posting run. 
  3. Invoice Posting: Supplier invoices are posted, leading to a reduction in accruals. 
  4. Year-End: After the accrual object's lifetime has ended, some supplier invoices remain unposted, leaving residual accruals. 
  5. Manual Release (January of next year): A Controller manually releases a partial amount of the existing accruals, deciding that the remaining accruals are too high and expecting lower costs from outstanding invoices. 
  6. Final Invoice Posting (February): The last supplier invoice is posted, utilizing some of the unreleased accruals. A small amount of accruals still remains. 
  7. Termination: With no further invoices expected, an accountant changes the accrual object status to 'F', automatically releasing the remaining accruals. This is done using the 'Edit -> Terminate Accrual Subobject Prematurely' action in transaction ACACTREE02. 

This sequence illustrates the typical lifecycle of a purchase order item, from accrual creation to final release. 

Manual Release of Accruals 

  • Manually releasing accruals is possible in transaction ACACTREE02: 
  • In this transaction the function Edit -> Release Accruals can be performed: 
  • A popup appears that allows to either fully or partially release accruals that had been posted before. 
  • In the partial release case, the user can manually enter the amount that shall be released. 

 

 

Manual Release of Accruals:  
With Accrual Closing Frequency 

Manually releasing accruals can result in both Release postings (transaction type RP) and Late Release postings (transaction type RL). For late release postings to occur, an accrual closing frequency must be specified in the customization settings of the accrual item type. 

Late release postings happen under the following conditions: 

  • The accruals were posted in a previous accrual closing period, typically in a prior fiscal year. 
  • The posting date for the release, as entered by the user in the release popup, is in a subsequent accrual closing period, such as the current fiscal year. 

If accruals were posted across multiple accrual closing periods, they will be displayed in the release popup as separate rows. The start and end dates of each accrual closing period (e.g., fiscal year) will be visible as columns. This format allows the user to decide which accrual amounts to release from each accrual closing period. 

Interplay of Utilization and Release Postings 

 

  • Usually, first utilization postings occur – and afterwards the remaining accruals are released. 
  • But the opposite sequence can also occur:  
  • A user has released accruals – and afterwards a utilization posting occurs. 
  • In this case, the utilization posting can lead to a reversal of the release posting.

entry.title: Meaning and Usage of Accrual Value Date
entry.content:
The Accrual Value Date is a specific field in a journal entry line item. Technically, this field is known as ACRVALDAT in the table ACDOCA. This field is populated exclusively in journal entry line items that are created by the Accrual Engine. It is utilized in all accrual-related postings performed by the Accrual Engine, such as periodic accrual postings, utilization, and/or release postings. Conversely, in all other types of postings, such as those recording actual costs from goods receipts or supplier invoices, this field remains empty and is not used.

 

The Accrual Engine uses the accrual value date to determine which accrual, utilization, or release amounts have already been posted for a specific period. Essentially, for entries created by the Accrual Engine, the accrual value date indicates the period to which these entries are assigned. By default, the Accrual Engine utilizes the posting date as the accrual value date.

Exceptions:

  • In certain posting screens of the Accrual Engine, it is possible to enter a posting date (or posting period and fiscal year) that differs from the accrual value date.
  • For Late Utilization (UL) and Late Release (RL, FL) postings, the accrual value date is different from the posting date. Such late postings can only occur if an Accrual Closing Frequency is employed (refer to the separate chapter for more details).

In summary: Suppose an invoice is posted in 2019 but utilizes accruals from 2018. When adding the utilization line items to the invoice, the information that these line items utilize accruals from the previous year (2018) must be stored in these line items. This information is stored in the new field called Accrual Value Date, which would be filled with the date 31.12.2018 in this case.

Since the posting date and document date are fields located in the header and are filled by the invoice itself—likely with a date in 2019—these fields cannot be used by the Accrual Engine. Consequently, a new field called Accrual Value Date had to be introduced in the journal entry line item.

entry.title: Latest People Profile Enablement: What You Need to Know
entry.content:
Introduction

This blog explains the points to be noted, key configurations to be done, and behaviours observed during the migration from the legacy People Profile to the latest People Profile.

Legacy People Profile to Latest People Profile Migration

Migration process:

To migrate configurations from the Legacy People Profile to the Latest People Profile: 

  • Navigate to Company System and Logo Settings >> Select “Migrate Legacy People Profile Configurations” >> Run the migration job  

With the 1H 2026 release, the Latest People Profile is automatically enabled, and the migration job is executed by default. However, administrators can still trigger the job manually if required. This change cannot be reverted to the Legacy People Profile.

Card Arrangement After Migration:

After the migration job is completed, both predefined and custom cards are reorganized into structured categories.

Predefined category:

Custom Category:

  • Custom blocks such as Live Profile Background Information, MDF Information, User Information, and Notes are moved to the Full Profile as custom cards.
  • These cards are organized into custom categories, keeping the same structure as in the old (legacy) profile layout.

System Limitations in Latest People Profile :

  • Maximum 20 custom categories allowed  
  • Maximum 30 cards per category  

Setting Category Access Control in Latest People Profile:

By default, the latest People Profile categories are visible to everyone. To restrict visibility based on roles, change the access to Role-Based Permissions.

Edit each category and configure the category access control.

 

Aligning Employee View Permissions Between Legacy and Latest People Profile

Navigate to Manage Permission Roles → Edit Role → Employee Views.

Under Employee Views, two categories are available:

  • People Profile Categories – used for the latest People Profile
  • Employee View Sections (only for Legacy People Profile) – used for the legacy People Profile

Refer to the permissions defined under Employee View Sections (only for Legacy People Profile) and provide similar permissions in People Profile Categories for each role.

You can also generate a report to review the list of roles and their existing legacy Employee View permissions and use it as a reference.

Set Header Fields in Latest People Profile:

During migration from legacy to the latest People Profile, header fields will not be migrated. They must be configured again in Configure Latest People Profile → General Settings → Configure Header Fields.

Common Issues and Behaviours in Latest People Profile Configuration:

  1. If there are duplicate sections or duplicate objects in the legacy People Profile during migration to the latest People Profile, only the first occurrence is considered, and the subsequent ones will not be displayed.
  2. Non-secured objects will not be displayed in the latest People Profile. For example, Advances may initially not be visible, so ensure the security for both Non-Recurring Payments and Advances objects is set to “Yes” in Configure Object Definitions.
  3. If the default screen in Configure Object Definition for objects to be displayed in the Employee Profile is not set, then even if the object permission is not granted to the employee, the card will still be displayed in their profile with an error. So, make sure to set it.
  4. If the compensation pie chart is not displayed in the employee’s Profile Compensation Information, it is because the employee does not have permission for the Pay Component Group.
  5. Recurring Deduction – even if permission is not granted, the card is displayed when there is no data. However, once data is entered, the card disappears, but there is no functional impact.
  6. If the Compensation Statement card is not visible in the Employee Profile, ensure that the necessary permission is granted for that in employee data.
  7. If any custom objects are set inside standard sections like Job Information, Compensation Information, etc., in the legacy People Profile, they will disappear during the latest People Profile migration and need to be set again in the relevant categories in the latest People Profile.
  8. In the legacy People Profile, the Org Chart was available under General Information. In the latest People Profile, the Org Chart is now available under Actions.

Conclusion

By May 15,16, 2026, the Latest People Profile will be automatically enabled in production. Therefore, ensure that all necessary configurations are completed, and the required permissions are granted.

Make sure to review legacy configurations and migrate any required settings to the latest People Profile categories. Validate role-based permissions to avoid missing or restricted visibility of cards and sections. Also, test the profile view to ensure all relevant data is displayed correctly after migration.

entry.title: How I transformed my SAPUI5 development from code to visual
entry.content:
Introduction

Ever wondered if there's some way to draw SAPUI5 freestyle screens? Well, that was possible back in the days and I miss it. And because of that, I decided to make it possible again.

 

The matter in hand

I've been working with SAPUI5 since 2020, developing freestyle apps in the SAP WEB IDE (good old days). I started when Fiori Elements wasn't as mature as it is today, with just SEGW as a tool to create oData services.

Nowadays we have Visual Studio Code, and it's great! It's lightweight and has so many advantages compared to the early Fiori development tools. But I always felt that some things didn't evolve like I wanted to. I feel some features are missing, like the visual editor:

 

Ok, it's true it's better to create the view using XML code. But everyone should remember many Fiori developers are also ABAP developers. And honestly, ABAP developers are not usually very UI-focused. We loved Module Pool (kinda).

So, I decided to create something to help me out and thought "Why not everyone else". With this, I built a small open-source tool to explore this idea. It originated standalone as a web app, but now it's fully integrated with Vscode.

The idea is simple. Design a screen with drag-and-drop:

Generate an XML:

And also save as file inside the project. 

 

The reverse is also possible: open an existing view with the visual editor:

Also, one thing that I emphasized is the properties inspector. You can edit properties from a component visually:

There are some presets when you want a quick boilerplate:

 

And then we can test it with the preview application:

Sure, it's flawed yet. The extension is in active development, but there are a lot of features that can help the day-to-day development:

  • Open a visual UI5 screen designer directly inside VS Code.
  • Build SAPUI5/OpenUI5 screen wireframes using visual components.
  • Add components by clicking or dragging them onto the canvas.
  • Move, resize, duplicate, copy, paste, and delete components.
  • Select and manipulate multiple components at once.
  • Edit component properties through a visual inspector.
  • Generate XML View code automatically from the visual layout.
  • Copy the generated XML to the clipboard.
  • Save the XML as a new file inside the workspace.
  • Open an existing XML View from the VS Code context menu.
  • Preserve or infer the View controllerName when generating XML.
  • Generate responsive layouts, avoiding fixed pixel widths where possible.
  • Use UI5 layout patterns such as VBox, HBox, SimpleForm, FlexItemData, percentages, and rem spacing.
  • Automatically detect required namespaces such as f, core, uxap, and unified.
  • Preserve common bindings when importing and regenerating XML, including table bindings.
  • Suggest bindings based on local OData metadata when a manifest.json and metadata file are available.
  • Run the whole generation flow locally, without sending XML or wireframes to external services.
  • Create empty UI5 view with corresponding controller with just 1 click, automatically.

The extension is still in preview/development. Generated XML should be reviewed before being used in production, especially for complex or highly customized UI5 Views.

The extension is called UI5 Designer 

And also, the project is open source on GitHub 

 

Conclusion

So that's that. The project is still on development, but for me that's something I could use already. I hope it can help everyone designing, prototyping and maintaining SAPUI5 Views easier and visually.

I'd love to hear from everyone, and also improve it for the community.

entry.content:
Hi Experts,

The report RODPS_REPL_SUBSCRIBER_ASSESS helps assess completed calls to the ODP‑RFC interface and identify potentially unpermitted usage by customer or third‑party applications. However, as noted, this report alone does not provide complete evidence of the non‑existence of unpermitted calls, and further manual investigation may be required.

Given this, what are the recommended ways or steps to obtain a comprehensive list of ODP‑RFC interfaces that are being used by non‑SAP external systems?
Are there standard tools, reports, or system logs or any other ways that can be leveraged to identify all such ODP‑RFC consumers reliably?

Snapshot is from Note: 3439624 

Any guidance or best practices would be appreciated. Thanks!

BR,
KRPK

BW Operational Data Provisioning (ODP)  BW Content and Extractors NW ABAP Remote Function Call (RFC)

entry.title: Re: Changing condition supplements doesn't create a workl...
entry.content:
Below are the steps to customize ADA for Condition Supplements

1) Check ADA Customizing Path
Go to SPRO → Materials Management → Purchasing → Conditions → Automatic Document Adjustment. Here you will find settings for Direct Entry for Creating Worklist (decides whether entries are created immediately or via batch job) and Document Index setup (tables like S111 and WIND are used to track relevant condition changes). By default, only gross price fields are flagged as relevant. Condition supplements are ignored unless you extend the logic.

2) Program RMEBEIN4 (Worklist Creation)
Report RMEBEIN4 reads change pointers (from BDCP2 with message type CONDBI) and compares them against customizing. If RA00 changes are not flagged as relevant, they won’t appear in the worklist. You can create a variant of RMEBEIN4 to include additional condition types, but this usually requires enhancement.

3) Enhancement Options

BAdI ME_INFORECORD_CUST or EXIT_SAPMM06I_012: Extend logic so that changes to RA00 (or other supplements) are treated as price-relevant.
Change Pointer Extension: Configure ALE change pointers to include RA00 condition changes, so ADA picks them up.
Custom Report: Some companies build a wrapper around RMEBEIN4 to add condition supplements into the worklist generation.

Practical Steps

  • Identify the condition type(s) you want ADA to monitor (e.g., RA00).
  • Extend the relevance check in the enhancement so that when RA00 changes, a pointer is written to the ADA worklist.
  • Test by changing RA00 in an info record and running RMEBEIN4 so that the entry should now appear.

entry.title: Re: 1E3: Custom Ordertyp for Kanban with external Procure...
entry.content:
Dear @ZBMA,

I verified this scenario in my Public Edition environment and in Central Business Configuration. The conclusion is that this requirement cannot be covered by standard CBC configuration.

In purchasing configuration, it is possible to create a customer-specific purchasing document type and assign a dedicated number range to it. SAP confirms that SSCUI 103412 is used to create purchase order number range intervals and SSCUI 102909 is used to assign the number range to a customized PO document type. SAP also confirms that document types in purchasing can be used to assign specific number ranges for special procurement processes

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3596398

However for Kanban external procurement the decisive configuration is the Kanban replenishment strategy. SAP confirms that when a Kanban is set to Empty the replenishment method depends on the strategy in the control cycle and for external procurement the replenishment is carried out via purchase orders. SAP also documents the Order Type field in Define External Procurement Strategies as the purchase order type for replenishment using purchase orders

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/configuring-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-manufacturing-execution/outlining-kanban

https://learning.sap.com/courses/configuring-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-manufacturing-execution/configuring-replenishment-strategies

I created and validated a dedicated PO document type with its own number range. However, in the Kanban external procurement strategy the Order Type field remains read-only in CBC and cannot be maintained. Because of this it cannot assign specific PO document type, such as a dedicated Kanban PO type to the external procurement Kanban strategy through standard CBC configuration

More details below (Please look at the last sentence):

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/a25ebd534f22b44ce10000000a174cb4.html

Therefore, a separate purchasing document type / separate number range specifically for Kanban external procurement orders cannot be delivered by standard configuration

Summary:

A dedicated PO document type and number range can be created in purchasing configuration but it cannot be assigned to the Kanban external procurement strategy in CBC because the relevant Order Type field is not maintainable. Therefore the requested Kanban-specific PO number range cannot be achieved by standard CBC configuration

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Enable Down Payment Process with Custom Sales Order Type
entry.content:
Background

As shared in a previous blog, it is possible to create custom sales order types in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. This new innovation provides greater flexibility to fulfill customer business requirements in the Order‑to‑Cash process.

In this blog, I will share another real business use case and explain how to utilize custom sales order types to address customer requirements.

Customer Requirement

Supply company XYZ sells various products to customers. For certain high-value orders, the company requires customers to make a down payment before the products are delivered. To support this, a billing plan must be enabled on the sales order so that the down payment amount can be defined directly in the order.

According to the flexible item category determination (see detail in help), customer's requirement can be fulfilled by decision table in BRF+ to determine the item category according to certain criteria. However, company XYZ prefers a separate sales order type to differentiate the normal sales process from the down payment sales process, while keeping the configuration clean and maintainable.

Solution

Let’s see how a custom sales order type fulfills this requirement.

Assume you are on SAP CBC and have already completed the activities “Define Scope” and “Set Up Organization Structure”. Open the activity “Configure Standard Order Type” to define a new custom sales order type.

Create a new custom sales order type by copying the standard type template XTA. Name the new type ZDP for the down payment sales process.

Save and confirm the custom sales order type. Then deploy the new custom sales order type to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Wait until the deployment is complete.

Once the custom sales order type ZDP is deployed, you need to configure the default item category for ZDP. Open the activity “Configure Your Business Processes” and search for SSCUI 102807 “Assign Item Categories”.

In SSCUI 102807, the default the item category is TAN, which is copied from standard order type OR.

Change the item category CBAO as the default item category for sales order type ZDP. Item category CBAO has billing plan type 90 (down payment) enabled, which allows a billing plan to be maintained at the item level in the sales order.

Save the configuration.

Now log on with user “Internal Sales Representative” and open the app “Create Sales Order”. Create a sales order with custom sales order type ZDP.

Add a sales order item. The system automatically determines item category CBAO for the item.

Navigate to the Billing Plan tab at the item level. You can see the billing plan tab is active, and you can define the down payment amount directly on the sales order item.

Save the sales order. 

Let’s compare with the standard sales order type “OR”. Create a sales order with order type OR and add an item.

The system determines item category TAN for the item.

Navigate to the item detail — there is no Billing Plan tab available for standard item category TAN.

This confirms that the down payment billing plan is available for the custom order type ZDP by default. 

Conclusion

The introduction of custom sales order types is a significant innovation that greatly enhances flexibility in the Order‑to‑Cash process. It allows customers to define their own sales processes with minimal configuration effort to support different sales scenarios and process patterns.

By combining a custom sales order type with a tailored item category assignment, company XYZ can now clearly differentiate between normal sales orders and down payment sales orders — with the billing plan automatically activated for the right process. This reduces configuration complexity while giving the business the process control it needs.

Please stay tuned for more custom sales order type use cases, and feel free to submit your requirements through the Customer Influence Portal to help us prioritize our product roadmap.

entry.title: Re: How do you handle tickets when no one on the team kno...
entry.content:
This is a very real pain point in many support projects and in such cases, my approach would be

1) User walkthroughs
Setting up a call and asking the user to reproduce the scenario in QA is a smart move. Watching the exact sequence often reveals subtle steps or assumptions that are not obvious from the ticket description. It can highlight misconfigurations, hidden dependencies, or even user misunderstandings. If needed, you can schedule Teams Call and record the session when the user is explaining their issue so that you can go through if needed at a later point of time

2) Replication clues
By mirroring the same sequence yourself, you can spot where the system diverges, which gives you a concrete starting point instead of blindly guessing.

3) Success case comparison
Another effective approach is to check a scenario where the process does work correctly. Comparing the failing case against a successful one highlights differences in configuration, data, or user behavior. This contrast often provides the missing context that documentation would have supplied.

4) Knowledge recovery
This approach effectively turns the user into a temporary “subject matter expert,” helping bridge the gap left by missing documentation.

5) Time impact
It can shorten resolution compared to pure reverse engineering, though it still tends to take longer than a well‑documented ticket. The payoff is that you often end up with clearer documentation afterward.

In fact, many teams I have seen adopt this as a standard practice. If the original builder is gone, the next best source of truth is the end user’s workflow. It is not perfect, but it’s often the most practical way to reconstruct intent. Based on the complexity of issue, you can involve ABAP team to narrow down the issue

entry.title: Date Override field not recognized in Compensation formula
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We have the following requirement in Compensation:

We are working with three fields:

  • Advancement Date: date field mapped from EC
  • Advancement Date Override: editable date field, ID customFinalAdvancementDateOverride
  • Final Advancement Date: calculated field

The intended logic for the final field is:

  • If customFinalAdvancementDateOverride is empty, populate with Advancement Date
  • Otherwise, populate with customFinalAdvancementDateOverride

However, this formula is not working as expected.

To troubleshoot, I created multiple test fields to check whether the system can recognize the editable override field:

  1. Direct reference to the override field
    • Date field with customFinalAdvancementDateOverride
    • String field with customFinalAdvancementDateOverride
    • Amount field with customFinalAdvancementDateOverride
  2. Using toNumber conversion
    • String field with toNumber(customFinalAdvancementDateOverride)
    • Amount field with toNumber(customFinalAdvancementDateOverride)
    • Date field with toNumber(customFinalAdvancementDateOverride)
  3. Using toString conversion
    • String field with toString(customFinalAdvancementDateOverride)
    • Amount field with toString(customFinalAdvancementDateOverride)
    • Date field with toString(customFinalAdvancementDateOverride)
  4. Using toDate conversion
    • Date field with toDate(customFinalAdvancementDateOverride,"MM/dd/yyyy")
    • String field with toDate(customFinalAdvancementDateOverride,"MM/dd/yyyy")
    • Amount field with toDate(customFinalAdvancementDateOverride,"MM/dd/yyyy")

All of these test fields are returning N/A.

The only exception is for the Date field and Amount field with a direct reference to customFinalAdvancementDateOverride. When selecting a date in the Advancement Date Override field, these fields still display N/A initially, but after saving the worksheet, the value is populated.

This behavior suggests that the editable override field value is not available in real time for formula evaluation and is only recognized after saving.

Based on this, I assume the editable override field may not be usable in formulas dynamically, or there may be a limitation related to how editable date fields are processed.

Has anyone experienced similar behavior or can confirm whether this requirement is feasible? Any suggestions would be appreciated.

Thank you.

 

In the attached screenshot, the fields highlighted in green show the behavior after saving the form, while the fields highlighted in red show the behavior before saving the form.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM Access via Non - SSO link
entry.content:
Dear customer,

based on the current setup, it appears that the identity provider configuration is in "state A." To optimize the configuration, we recommend upgrading to "state B," provided this aligns with your requirements. Before proceeding, we advise carefully reviewing the implications outlined in the documentation available here: Identity Provider Configuration Guide.  

Once the upgrade to "state B" is completed, it will enable the ability to log on specific users directly via IAS using their username and password, such as e. g., while other users can be redirected to Azure AD by default. Detailed guidance on this functionality is explained under "state B" in the section titled Allow Identity Authentication Users Log On.  

Best Regards,  
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Mapping Possibilities for Current Z MRP (VM + Forecas...
entry.content:
Below are the options you can explore:

  • PD (standard MRP) → Pure requirements-based; forecast not considered.
  • Reorder point MRP without forecast → Stock-driven only; forecast ignored.
  • Forecast → PIRs → Forecasts converted into Planned Independent Requirements; MRP runs against PIRs. This preserves forecast-driven procurement quantities and is the closest standard mapping.
  • DDMRP → Buffer-based planning; strategic alternative but different logic.
  • Custom enhancement → Needed if hybrid behavior is business-critical.

Recommendation is to Convert forecast “G” into PIRs and use PD for planning, with reorder point maintained as safety stock. Run pilots to validate behavior. If hybrid logic is essential, consider a controlled custom enhancement.

Given below is the key migration options in S/4HANA
